items.title: Zahid proposes Orang Asli officer at Negri Sembilan MB office
items.summary: JELUBU: A special officer representing the Orang Asli community could be appointed at the Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar's Office without the need for a manifesto pledge, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said the proposal was raised by the Orang Asli community during his visit to Kampung Orang Asli Tohor here today and described the request as reasonable. Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional chairman, said appointing a special officer would strengthen direct engagement between Orang Asli leaders and the Menteri Besar's Office.

items.title: Zahid: BN-PN understanding needs time before expanding to other polls
items.summary: JELEBU: Barisan Nasional's electoral understanding with Perikatan Nasional in Negri Sembilan should be allowed to develop organically before being expanded to other state elections, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The BN chairman said any decision to expand the cooperation would depend on the outcome of the Negri Sembilan election first. He said the understanding between the two coalitions was still in its early stages and should be allowed to mature naturally based on sincerity and mutual understanding.

items.title: How TikTok is becoming Malaysia’s newsroom — Marissa Mae
items.summary: According to the Reuters Institute’s Digital News Report, TikTok has become the fastest-growing platform for news consumption worldwide. In 2024, around 31 per cent of Malaysians said they used TikTok to access news. Among people aged 18 to 24, social media has overtaken traditional news outlets, with 44 per cent saying it is now their main source of news.

items.title: Man jailed 60 years, ordered 60 strokes of rotan over daughter rape case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 56-year-old Indonesian man was sentenced by the Sessions Court today to 60 years' imprisonment and 60 strokes of the rotan after pleading guilty to six counts of raping his biological daughter, resulting in her giving birth to a baby girl. Judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid ordered the man to serve 10 years' imprisonment and receive 10 strokes of the rotan for each charge, with the jail terms to run consecutively from the date of his arrest yesterday. A DNA test confirmed that the accused was the baby's biological father.

items.title: Oil nears US$100 a barrel as Middle East risks deepen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Renewed tensions in the Middle East have pushed Brent crude prices back towards US$100 a barrel, with markets pricing in growing risks of prolonged supply disruptions and tighter global energy flows. OCBC said Brent futures are now trading above its forecasts of US$75 a barrel in 4Q26 and US$71 a barrel by mid-2027, after briefly retreating towards pre-conflict levels near US$70 a barrel. The renewed conflict has disrupted oil shipping routes, with a fresh US blockade of Iranian ports adding pressure on exports and delaying expectations of supply normalisation, Sim added.

items.title: PSD introduces Touch 'n Go e-Wallet pension payments, expands MyPesara Plus
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A total of 835,146 pensioners and derivative pension beneficiaries will benefit from two new Public Service Department (PSD) initiatives, namely pension payment channels through the Touch 'n Go e-Wallet and expanded MyPesara Plus benefits. He said the initiative, developed through collaboration between the PSD, the Ministry of Finance, Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) and Touch 'n Go e-Wallet, would ensure retirees continue to receive their pension payments without disruption through a more flexible and secure channel. Meanwhile, Wan Ahmad Dahlan said the MyPesara Plus initiative expanded the Pensioner Benefits Programme by transforming the pensioner card into more than just an identification document, allowing retirees to enjoy various benefits through strategic partnerships with the private sector.

items.title: Asia's tech exports cushioned from tariff blow
items.summary: The United States has imposed new tariffs ranging from 10 per cent to 12.5 per cent on imports from 60 trading partners under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974 effective today, replacing the temporary 10 per cent tariff that expired on July 24. Malaysia is among 17 economies placed in the lower 10 per cent tariff category, alongside Indonesia, India, Canada, Mexico and the United Kingdom. Other economies, including China, Singapore and Australia, face tariffs of 12.5 per cent, while Japan and South Korea are also subject to 12.5 per cent tariffs, with some relief available under existing trade agreements.

items.title: K9 unit joins probe into Selangor Wholesale Market fire [WATCH]
items.summary: SERDANG: The Fire and Rescue Department's K9 unit was deployed to the Selangor Wholesale Market as forensic officers began investigating the fire that destroyed about 90 per cent of the premises. Earlier, Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said an excavator arrived at about noon to clear debris. The operation involved 68 fire and rescue personnel, five Fire Rescue Tenders, one Light Fire Rescue Tender, two Aerial Ladder Platforms, one Bronto Aerial Tower Vehicle, three Emergency Medical Rescue Services units and seven water tankers.

items.title: Proton opens 10 outlets in 10 days
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Proton Holdings Bhd has expanded its network with the opening of 10 new Proton and Proton e.Mas outlets within 10 days, strengthening customer access to its products and aftersales services nationwide. As of June, Proton's nationwide network comprised 195 sales outlets, including 178 integrated 3S and 4S centres. Proton deputy chief executive officer Datuk Ir Abdul Rashid Musa said the 10 new outlets underscores the close collaboration between Proton, Proton e.Mas and its dealer partners.

items.title: Going durian crazy at Grab's Kaw Kaw Fest
items.summary: Powered by Grab Dine Out, the festival will run from 4pm to 11pm daily. The event aims to showcase the synergy within the Grab ecosystem, bringing together services such as Grab Dine Out, Grab Mart and the platform's strategic partnership with Jaya Grocer. This allows the festival to demonstrate Grab's grocery delivery capabilities alongside its dine-in and takeaway offerings.

items.title: Witness tells court of RM20.3 million transfers from PNB to FashionValet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court was told today that there were two fund transfers totalling RM20.3 million from Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB) to Fashion Valet Sdn Bhd. Maybank Jalan Tun Razak branch assistant manager Shamsiah Musa, 55, said the two transactions were made on June 4, 2018, and Dec 20, 2018. I confirm that there were only two transfers from PNB's Malayan Banking Bhd account to Fashion Valet Sdn Bhd's Public Bank account," she said.

items.title: ‘I have made mistakes, but do not slander me’: PM Anwar hits out at false claims during Negeri Sembilan campaign
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, July 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged all parties to refrain from spreading slander and false information for political gain, particularly during the campaign for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. Anwar said he had himself been the target of false claims, including allegations that he had convened a meeting to spread propaganda among serving military personnel and veterans while campaigning for the election. Also present were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari, Chief of Defence Forces General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman and Army chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman.

items.title: Animal group seeks transparency in Bentong animal cruelty case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Department of Veterinary Services has been urged to provide a transparent update on enforcement action taken over an animal neglect and cruelty case in Kampung Chemomoi, Bentong. The Stray Animal Foundation Malaysia questioned whether the department had officially opened an investigation paper and identified those responsible for the alleged abuse. The situation clearly demonstrates severe neglect and animal cruelty.

items.title: Defence Ministry tightens grenade training safety after Hobart camp tragedy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Defence Ministry and armed forces will tighten safety measures for grenade training after an investigation into last month's fatal explosion at Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the measures would be implemented to reduce the risk of similar incidents recurring. Khaled said that the safety of military personnel would remain the ministry's highest priority.

items.title: PNB imposed no restrictions on RM20.3m transferred to FashionValet, witness tells court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — The Sessions Court was told today that there were two fund transfers totalling RM20.3 million from Permodalan Nasional Berhad (PNB) to Fashion Valet Sdn Bhd. Maybank Jalan Tun Razak branch assistant manager Shamsiah Musa, 55, said the two transactions were made on June 4, 2018, and December 20, 2018. I confirm that there were only two transfers from PNB’s Malayan Banking Berhad account to Fashion Valet Sdn Bhd’s Public Bank account,” she said.

items.title: Selangor Wholesale Market redevelopment to include enhanced fire safety features
items.summary: SERDANG: The Selangor Wholesale Market is expected to undergo redevelopment early next year, with enhanced safety features incorporated into its new design. Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the redevelopment plan was being finalised, with construction expected to begin in 2027. Izham, who is also the Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture exco member, said safety would be a key priority in the new design, including improved firefighting systems, adequate water supply for firefighting purposes, sprinkler systems and sufficient parking facilities.

items.title: ICC prosecutor Karim Khan's future hangs in the balance over sexual assault allegations
items.summary: THE HAGUE: Karim Khan will go down in the history of the International Criminal Court as the prosecutor who clinched arrest warrants against the leaders of Russia and Israel but also as the one who "poisoned the atmosphere" within the institution. At his swearing-in ceremony in 2021 as chief prosecutor of the Hague-based court, Khan said the ICC should be judged on its actions: "The proof of the pudding should be in the eating." Asked about "crossing the floor" – working as both prosecutor and defence – Khan told specialist journal OpinioJuris it helps lawyers stay "grounded."

items.title: Onn Hafiz defends Johor's 10 deputy exco appointments
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has defended the appointment of 10 deputy executive councillors, saying the move is lawful, administrative in nature and does not create new executive powers or alter the state's governing structure. Responding to criticism from several Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders, Onn Hafiz said the appointments were made within the state government's administrative powers and based on advice from the Johor State Legal Adviser. He said the deputy exco members were not part of the state executive council and therefore did not possess any decision-making authority.

items.title: MACC officer rejects claim Penang tunnel land deal caused no loss
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The investigating officer in the Penang undersea tunnel corruption trial today rejected a defence claim that the state government suffered no losses after transferring state land to pay for the project's feasibility study. CZBUCG was awarded the RM6.34 billion Penang undersea tunnel and major roads project. Haijan: The state government has already paid for the work carried out by CZBUCG through a RM135 million land swap.

items.title: Local logistics firms squeezed by Chinese rivals' aggressive pricing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Local logistics companies are facing mounting pressure from Chinese competitors offering what Kenanga Research described as "irrational pricing", even as operating costs continue to rise. The research house said aggressive pricing by Chinese logistics firms is limiting the ability of local players, including Swift Haulage Bhd, to benefit fully from Malaysia's robust trade growth. Kenanga Research said many Chinese logistics firms enter Malaysia alongside Chinese foreign direct investments, particularly when new manufacturing plants are established.

items.title: Samenta says even 10pc US tariff inflicts severe strain on local exporters
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — The Small and Medium Enterprises Association of Malaysia (Samenta) is calling for targeted relief measures for local exporters, warning that the newly implemented 10 per cent US tariff inflicts an immediate operational strain on SMEs. While the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti) highlighted that Malaysia secured a comparatively favourable rate compared to the 12.5 per cent imposed on several regional peers, Samenta argues that this distinction offers little comfort to small businesses. The association stressed that the tariff acts as a sovereign-level regulatory penalty tied to national enforcement gaps, meaning compliant SMEs that have invested heavily in international labour and ESG standards are now collateral damage in a broader trade war.

items.title: Three shops affected by Selangor Wholesale Market fire, others remain open
items.summary: SERDANG: Businesses unaffected by the fire at the Selangor Wholesale Market have continued operating as usual after the blaze damaged part of the market last night. Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the incident only involved three premises, while other traders at the market continued their operations. "The fire involved three shops - one selling catering equipment, another selling eggs and a hardware shop.

items.title: Marine police bust international drug syndicate, intercept RM6.1 million haul
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Marine police have crippled an international drug smuggling attempt after intercepting a fibre boat carrying drugs worth RM6.1 million in waters off Pulau Rimau here. Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri said the success was the result of intelligence-led enforcement by the Marine Police Region One Penang during Op Gelora 190/2026 on July 17. He said the operation was launched after the Marine Police intelligence unit detected suspicious movements involving a fibre boat believed to have been used by a drug trafficking syndicate to smuggle narcotics through Malaysian waters.

items.title: South Korean AI chip billionaire ordered to pay US$644m to ex-wife in divorce
items.summary: SEOUL: A South Korean court ordered an AI chip-manufacturing tycoon on Friday to make a trimmed-down US$644 million divorce payment to his former wife. Chey Tae-won, 65, is the chairman of the SK Group, which owns world-leading memory chipmaker SK hynix – a pillar of South Korea's tech-led economy. Chey appealed to the Supreme Court, which told the high court to reconsider its 2024 decision and led to the tycoon's reduced divorce bill of 944 billion won (US$644 million).

items.title: BN-PN pact will not change coalition's multiracial stance, says Asyraf
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: Barisan Nasional's (BN) understanding with Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Negri Sembilan election will not change the coalition's commitment to protecting and serving all Malaysians, said Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. He said BN remained a coalition dedicated to safeguarding the welfare of all Malaysians, regardless of race or religion. He said since BN's formation, its component parties - Umno, MCA and MIC - had proven that racial diversity could be managed harmoniously.

items.title: Selangor Sultan attends Yasin recital in conjunction with Warriors' Day
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, today attended the 'yasin' recital, 'doa selamat' prayers and 'tahlil' at the Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Mosque here, in conjunction with the state-level Warriors Day celebration. The event, which was led by Selangor Deputy Mufti Datuk Dr Miszairi Sitiris, was also attended by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari; Islamic Affairs and Innovation Committee chairman Dr Mohammad Fahmi Ngah; Youth, Sports and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman Mohd Najwan Halimi and Selangor Islamic Religious Department (Jais) director Datuk Mohd Shahzihan Ahmad. The congregation then performed the Friday prayers led by Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Mosque Grand Imam Datuk Muhammad Farhan Wijaya, who delivered a sermon titled 'Hakikat Kepahlawanan Islam'.

items.title: China opposes new US tariffs, warns against trade wars
items.summary: BEIJING: Beijing condemned fresh US tariffs imposed on China and 59 other countries over alleged forced labour concerns Friday, and warned Washington against waging a trade war. The US levies range from 10 to 12.5 per cent, with China slapped with the highest rate. The new tariffs replace an expiring global duty rolled out by President Donald Trump earlier this year.

items.title: KL police intensify action against begging syndicates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) is stepping up efforts to combat crime in Kuala Lumpur, including syndicates exploiting organised begging, through initiatives such as establishing new police posts with support from the corporate sector. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said organised begging remains one of the longstanding challenges facing police in maintaining public safety and order in the capital. Meanwhile, Fadil said Kuala Lumpur Police currently operate 25 police stations and 28 police posts across six police districts, including Putrajaya.

items.title: Court dismisses RM110mil suit by China businessman over 51 luxury units
items.summary: High Court judge Datuk Anand Ponnudurai dismissed Cheah Ting Feng's suit against Lumayan Indah Sdn Bhd and 1 Pavilion Property Consultancy Sdn Bhd, ruling that he had failed to prove he was induced by any fraudulent or negligent misrepresentation. The court also found that Cheah had failed to establish any breach of contract or illegality that would justify rescinding the sale and purchase agreements (SPAs) for the units at Banyan Tree Signatures in Jalan Conlay. Cheah had sought to rescind all 51 SPAs executed in April 2019, recover the RM110 million purchase price and obtain damages, alleging he had been promised the units could be operated as hotel or short-term rental accommodation with guaranteed rental returns.

items.title: Honda Malaysia powers up Melaka plant with solar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Honda Malaysia Sdn Bhd has completed its first solar power installation project at its manufacturing plant in Melaka, marking a big step in its sustainability journey and long-term goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2050. The company said the initiative reflects its commitment to integrating sustainable practices into its manufacturing operations while supporting Malaysia's renewable energy agenda. The solar power system consists of about 8,960 solar panels installed across the rooftops of the Melaka plant, with a total generation capacity of more than 5.6 megawatts.

items.title: Anwar: Another Petronas tanker set to arrive in Malaysia next month
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: Another Petronas tanker that has been granted clearance to transit the Strait of Hormuz is expected to arrive in Melaka early next month, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said Petroliam Nasional Bhd, president and group chief executive officer, Tengku Tan Sri Muhammad Taufik had informed him of the latest development yesterday. Anwar said the vessel's successful passage reflected Malaysia's prudent foreign policy, particularly its ability to maintain good diplomatic relations with Iran.

items.title: Malaysia bars Israelis due to oppression, not anti-Semitism, says Anwar
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, July 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today stressed that Malaysia’s decision to bar Israeli citizens from entering the country was not driven by anti-Jewish sentiments, but rather by its stance against Israel’s acts of oppression and occupation. He said the claim that Malaysia was anti-Jewish was untrue as the country’s position was based on Israel’s actions in oppressing the Palestinian people. He said Malaysia would not budge on the issue of barring the entry of Israeli citizens despite pressure from United States lawmakers.

items.title: Ukraine hits 'Russia's Amazon' warehouses outside St Petersburg
items.summary: SAINT PETERSBURG: Ukrainian drones Friday hit warehouses for Russian e-commerce giant Wildberries for a third time in six days, this time near Saint Petersburg, triggering a fire that sent smoke pouring over the historic city. Ukraine has targeted Saint Petersburg as it steps up long-range drone strikes on Russian energy, military and logistics sites in recent weeks, calling the attacks justified retribution for Moscow's nightly drone and missile barrage of its cities. Wildberries said two of its logistics facilities in Saint Petersburg and one in Simferopol, in Moscow-annexed Crimea, were hit.

items.title: Malaysia remains in lower US tariff tier
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia remains among the 17 economies subject to the US' lower 10 per cent Section 301 tariff following Washington's investigations into forced labour-related import enforcement across 60 economies. The tariff, which took effect on July 24, follows the expiry of the temporary tariff imposed under Section 122 of the US Trade Act of 1974. The ministry also said the USTR's Section 301 investigation into excess capacity, involving 16 economies including Malaysia, remains ongoing.

items.title: Miti: Malaysia escapes steeper tariff, but a second US probe looms over ‘excess capacity’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Malaysia will face a 10 per cent tariff imposed by the United States under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974, following a broad investigation into forced labour practices across dozens of trading economies. The Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti) confirmed today that Malaysia is one of 17 economies to qualify for the lower 10 per cent rate, while other targeted economies will be subject to a steeper 12.5 per cent tariff. This more favourable rate acknowledges the United States Trade Representative’s (USTR) recognition of Malaysia’s commitments to implement and enforce laws prohibiting the importation of goods made with forced labour.

items.title: Thousands flee wildfires in southern Europe
items.summary: LÈGE-CAP-FERRET, France: Luggage-clad residents and tourists fled a French peninsula by boat and the smell of smoke drifted into parts of Madrid on Friday as wildfires swept through parts of western Europe, triggering states of emergencies and calls for EU help. In France, fires raging near the country's prized wine country of Bordeaux forced hundreds of people to flee the Cap Ferret peninsula in the middle of the summer vacation season as the blaze devoured an area nearly twice the size of Manhattan. The EU said it would provide three water-bombing planes to France.

items.title: PM Anwar: Tabung Haji RCI report kept under wraps to avoid public panic, withdrawals
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, July 24 — The Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) report on Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) was not released three years ago because the government wanted to avoid undermining public confidence in the pilgrims’ fund board, according to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said publishing the report at the time could have triggered panic among depositors, leading to a mass withdrawal of funds that might have destabilised TH. Also present were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari, Chief of Defence Forces General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman and Army chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman.

items.title: CIMB anchors Malaysia's US$1.5bil global sukuk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Group Holdings Bhd has strengthened its debt capital markets position as the sole Malaysian joint lead manager and joint bookrunner for the government's US$1.5 billion global sukuk issuance. CIMB said its appointment reflected the company's strong domestic debt capital markets franchise, global distribution capabilities and leadership in Islamic finance. Group chief executive officer Novan Amirudin said the bank's role reflects the strength of its debt capital markets platform, global distribution capabilities and longstanding leadership in Islamic finance.

items.title: ‘No prima facie case’: Chegubard acquitted of sedition charge linked to post on King
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — The Sessions Court today acquitted activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, of a sedition charge over a 2024 Facebook post. Judge Siti Aminah Ghazali made the ruling after finding that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case against Badrul Hisham, 48, at the close of its case. “The court finds that there is no prima facie case.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia to continue US tariff talks, relieved by relatively low rate
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, July 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government will continue negotiations with the United States over the new tariffs imposed on Malaysia related to the forced labour issue if the outcome remains unsatisfactory. He said the government has taken note of the latest US announcement on the tariff rates, expressing relief that the rates imposed on Malaysia are relatively low. Also present were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Chief of Defence Forces General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman, Army Chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman, and Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari.

items.title: Amirudin to BN: Don’t let Perikatan swallow you in pursuit of ‘ummah unity’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Pakatan Harapan election director Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari today told Barisan Nasional to be wary of being “swallowed” by Perikatan Nasional in pursuit of Malay-Muslim “ummah unity”, arguing that previous attempts to bring PAS and Umno together have repeatedly ended in failure. He said signs that PN was seeking to dominate BN were already emerging, pointing to Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin’s proposal to call their cooperation “Perikatan Barisan Nasional”, a name that several Umno leaders have since distanced themselves from. Amirudin also questioned PAS’s argument that “ummah unity” would translate into better governance and economic outcomes, pointing to states where PN and PAS enjoy overwhelming political dominance.

items.title: Gemas-Mentakab rail upgrade completed at RM41.7m, says Loke
items.summary: “This project is important to ensure the continuity of the East Coast railway route as it spans several states, namely Negeri Sembilan, Pahang and Kelantan. Therefore, this is a rail artery connecting the East Coast and West Coast via Gemas. Loke, who is also Seremban MP, said Gemas has now become an important interchange hub for the country’s rail system as it serves as the meeting point for the East Coast and West Coast routes.

items.title: Mastermind among 11 charged with being members of ‘Vinod Gang’ crime syndicate in Sepang
items.summary: SEPANG, July 24 — Eleven men were brought before the Sessions Court here today on charges of being members of an organised crime syndicate known as “Vinod Gang.” No plea was recorded after the charges were read before Judge Husna Dzulkifly, as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. Earlier, all 11 accused arrived at the court compound around 9 am under heavy security from the Royal Malaysia Police’s Criminal Investigation Department (CID). In a statement yesterday, Bukit Aman CID director Datuk M Kumar said the group is believed to have been active since 2023 and involved in gang robberies, attempted murder, armed rioting, causing injury with weapons and mischief.

items.title: Hadi: Umno’s Malay supremacy and PAS’s Islamic supremacy are two sides of the same coin
items.summary: MARANG, July 24 — The concepts of “Malay supremacy” (Ketuanan Melayu) and “Islamic supremacy” (Ketuanan Islam) will not trigger an ideological clash in the political cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN). Instead, PAS President Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang claimed the two visions can be seamlessly integrated. Speaking after a Duha lecture at Rusila Mosque on Friday, Abdul Hadi argued that the fundamental principles of Malay identity and Islam are complementary and inseparable, Sinar Harian reported.

items.title: ‘Destined to sink’: Hannah Yeoh likens BN-PN alliance to the Titanic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — DAP deputy secretary-general Hannah Yeoh has warned that the emerging cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) is destined to collapse, comparing the alliance to the doomed Titanic. “I likened their alliance to the ship in the film Titanic, a vessel destined to sink sooner or later,” Yeoh said, recounting her speech at a Pakatan Harapan (PH) campaign event in Kuala Klawang, Jelebu. Yeoh was campaigning for incumbent Klawang assemblyman Datuk Bakri Sawir, who is seeking to retain the seat for PH.

items.title: Johor police arrest three, seize 71 Bitcoin mining machines in TNB power theft bust
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 24 — Johor police have busted a syndicate illegally mining Bitcoin using electricity stolen from Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB), following four raids on July 22 and 23. Police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the operation, codenamed Ops Letrik, was carried out by the Johor Contingent Police Headquarters’ Criminal Investigation Department (D4), working alongside TNB’s Southern Region SEAL team. During a press conference at the Johor Contingent Police Headquarters today, he said the items seized included 71 cryptocurrency mining machines, two computers, two laptops, five routers, two monitors, two computer keyboards, a mobile phone and two vehicles.

items.title: ‘Only Tok Min’: Loke says Aminuddin is PH’s sole Negeri Sembilan MB candidate
items.summary: JEMPOL, July 24 — Negeri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) remains unanimous in backing Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun as its sole candidate for Menteri Besar should the coalition retain power in the state election, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said. Loke, who is also Negeri Sembilan DAP chairman, said no other candidate was being considered for the post besides Aminuddin, who also chairs Negeri Sembilan PH. “Our Menteri Besar candidate is only one person, that is Tok Min (Aminuddin).

items.title: In food security push, Penang disburses RM3.28m in aid to rice and vegetable farmers
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, July 24 — The Penang state government is disbursing a one-off RM500 cash grant to 6,572 padi and vegetable farmers to alleviate the cost of living and bolster the state’s food supply chain. The aid will benefit 5,567 padi farmers and 1,005 vegetable growers across the state. Meanwhile, the state’s vegetable farmers produced 10,648 metric tonnes in 2025.

items.title: ‘Sepucuk Surat Sebuah Episod’ offers rare glimpse into letters from Malaysia’s prominent figures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — In his latest book, Sepucuk Surat Sebuah Episod: Rakaman Pelaku-Pelaku Zaman , celebrated cultural figure Raja Ahmad Aminullah brings together personal letters exchanged with some of Malaysia’s key figures, offering a glimpse into the people who helped shape the nation’s story. Raja Ahmad, fondly known as Pak Engku, said the collection of personal correspondence and exclusive interactions was not intended as a political book, but as a socio-cultural documentation of his observations on the communities around him. The book was launched by the National Book Council of Malaysia (MBKM) director Mohd Khair Ngadiron, with the event attended by artists, literary figures, academics and Raja Ahmad’s long-time associates.

items.title: Police detain several after student and father attacked outside Serdang school
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Police have detained several individuals to assist investigations into the recent assault of a 16-year-old student and his father outside a school in Taman Desaminium, Serdang following the circulation of a video of the incident on social media. Serdang district police chief ACP Muhamad Farid Ahmad today said the incident occurred at about 12.45pm on July 3 when the student of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Taman Desaminium was called over by several male students from the same school while walking towards his motorcycle outside the school compound. Prior to this, a video purportedly showing the incident, in which several secondary school students attacked a man at a motorcycle parking area near the school, went viral on social media, drawing widespread public attention.

items.title: ‘We can fix this’: BN touts itself as mediator for Negeri Sembilan royal dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has positioned itself as the key to resolving the ongoing royal dispute in Negeri Sembilan, claiming that a mandate to govern the state would allow the coalition to facilitate the best possible resolution between Yang Dipertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, and the state’s four Undang. Speaking on the KiniTV “Explain Sikit” podcast, state BN chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias urged voters to consider the state’s stability before casting their ballots on August 1. He argued that the current royal dispute has spiralled into a contentious deadlock that requires expert crisis management.

items.title: Police arrest man over attempted cash van robbery in Klang, recover stolen shotgun
items.summary: Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said the suspect, in his 20s, was arrested in Shah Alam, Selangor, at about 11am yesterday. He said the suspect had allegedly attempted to rob a security van transporting cash before seizing a pump-action shotgun from a security guard and fleeing on a motorcycle. Kumar said checks showed the suspect had a previous record under Section 8 of the Arms Act 1960.

items.title: NGOs to protest at US Embassy over interference in Malaysia’s sovereign policies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Three non-governmental organisations (NGOs) will hold a peaceful assembly and submit a memorandum of protest to the United States (US) Embassy here today to oppose the attempt by US members of Congress to pressurise Malaysia over its sovereign policies. The three organisations are the Malaysian Consultative Council of Islamic Organisations (Mapim), the Muslim Youth Movement of Malaysia (ABIM) and Pertubuhan Kebajikan Rakyat Sejagat Malaysia (Sejagat). The gathering is themed “Rakyat Bersatu Tolak Campur Tangan AS” (The People United Against US Interference) and carries the slogan “Pertahankan Kedaulatan Negara” (Defend National Sovereignty).

items.title: Guan Eng moots MSME relief, education reform in letter to Anwar
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 24 — Former minister Lim Guan Eng has presented a strategic roadmap to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, outlining six critical interventions designed to safeguard the wellbeing of the people and drive Malaysia’s future economic progress. Underpinning the proposal is a call for urgent financial relief for micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs). Beyond financial relief, Lim called for a digital overhaul of foreign worker processing.

items.title: Major fire erupts at Seri Kembangan wholesale market, gas tanks explode
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Division Assistant Director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said that five nearby fire hydrants were unusable because they were under private management, forcing crews to rely entirely on water from fire engines and tanker lorries, BH reported. The fire, believed to have started at approximately 10:50 pm, spread rapidly due to strong winds. A massive contingent of 68 JBPM officers and 67 Fire Volunteer members were deployed to the scene.

items.title: Malaysian passport ranks world’s seventh most powerful, ahead of US, says index
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Malaysia’s passport remained the world’s seventh most powerful in the Henley Passport Index 2026, offering visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to 183 destinations. According to the index, Malaysia shared seventh place with Australia, Canada, Czechia, Latvia, New Zealand, Slovakia and Slovenia. Brunei was ranked 19th with access to 163 destinations.

items.title: New charges prompt removal of containers left up to 407 days at Sepanggar Port
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 24 — Firm action by the Sabah state government and implementation of new port charges have addressed the issue of abandoned containers at Sepanggar Port here, the Sabah State Legislative Assembly (DUN) was told yesterday. He added that the amendment also aims, among others, to harmonise the definition of ‘Minister’ under the Sabah Ports (Privatisation) Enactment 1998 with the Sabah Ports Authority Enactment 1981. Meanwhile, the sitting also passed the Sabah Native Affairs Council (Amendment) Bill 2026 and the District Chief, Native Chief and Headman Bill 2026, tabled by Sabah Local Government and Housing Minister Datuk Dr Mohd Arifin Mohd Arif.

items.title: ‘Never heard of it’: Sanusi shrugs off Hamzah’s ‘PBN’ label
items.summary: JEMPOL, July 24 — Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor has dismissed claims of any official discussion to formalise the electoral alliance between Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Barisan Nasional (BN) as “Perikatan Barisan Nasional” (PBN). The proposal, put forward by Parti Wawasan Negara President Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, was brushed aside by Sanusi, who also serves as PAS’s election director. There hasn’t even been any discussion at that level,” Sanusi told reporters during a PN and BN rally for the Jempol constituency yesterday.

items.title: Anwar: Indian community empowerment must focus on education, skills and jobs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Efforts to empower the Indian community must prioritise quality education, comprehensive welfare, skills development and better employment opportunities, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. In a Facebook post yesterday, Anwar said he emphasised these priorities during a meeting with Indian community leaders. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, urged the Malaysian Indian Transformation Unit (MITRA) and all related agencies to take a more proactive approach at the grassroots level.

items.title: Bumiputera petrol station operators urge review of credit card charges as fuel costs rise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — The Bumiputera Petrol Station Operators Association of Malaysia (Bumipeda) has urged the government to review the Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) charged on credit card transactions. Its vice-president, Shahrul Nizam Md Radzi, said MDR charges are placing an increasing burden on petrol station operators, adding to the cost pressures they already face from rising fuel prices. “Therefore, sound policies should not only protect consumers but also ensure the entire supply chain remains sustainable,” he said.
